title: Edmonton's Housing Market Is Picking Up Steam But It's Not the Market Sellers Remember
excerpt: Edmonton recorded 2,133 home sales in March a 33% jump from February but year-over-year numbers tell a different story, with inventory 31.6% higher than this time last year and sales still running below 2025 levels.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Edmonton Residents Honoured After Saving Stranger’s Life During Medical Emergency
excerpt: Two Edmonton community members are being recognized by Alberta Health Services after their quick thinking and immediate action helped save the life of a stranger who collapsed behind their workplace.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Edmonton's Butterdome Goes Viral After Being Called the Ugliest Building in Canada
excerpt: The iconic yellow pavilion on the University of Alberta campus has always divided opinions but a viral post with nearly 280,000 views just made the Butterdome a talking point far beyond Edmonton.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Explosive Device Found in Sherwood Park Car May Have Been Designed to Kill Multiple People
excerpt: A routine traffic stop in Strathcona County may have prevented a potentially deadly incident after RCMP officers discovered a homemade explosive device inside a vehicle.

title: Why Edmonton Is Becoming One of Canada’s Fastest-Growing Event Cities
excerpt: Edmonton is emerging as a major event city in Canada, from festivals to international competitions. Here’s why more events are choosing Edmonton.

title: Edmonton Man Charged With Human Trafficking, Bestiality After Lengthy ALERT Investigation
excerpt: An Edmonton man is facing 26 criminal charges after investigators allege he trafficked and exploited multiple women in the city, with additional disturbing allegations involving animal abuse.

title: Edmonton Police Warn of "Bank Courier" Scam After Toronto Man Arrested in Edmonton
excerpt: Suspects claiming to be official bank representatives are calling Edmontonians and stating that their bank cards have been compromised, and their cards are required to be placed in an envelope for collection. A driver then attends their residence and collects the package
author: Culture Alberta

title: Edmonton Police Investigating Suspicious Death of 52-Year-Old Man in West Edmonton, Male Youth Arrested
excerpt: Edmonton Police are investigating the death of a 52-year-old man after officers were called to a residence near West Edmonton Mall on Friday evening. A male youth was arrested at the scene and the EPS Homicide Section has taken over the investigation.

title: RCMP Seeking Public Help to Find Missing 13-Year-Old Alexis Ermineskin-Crier from Wetaskiwin
excerpt: Wetaskiwin RCMP and Alexis Ermineskin-Crier's family are asking for the public's help locating a 13-year-old girl last seen in Wetaskiwin on April 3, 2026.

title: Canada's First Indigenous Film Festival Returns to Edmonton April 15 Here's What to Know About Dreamspeakers 2026
excerpt: The Dreamspeakers International Indigenous Film Festival returns to Edmonton April 15 to 19, featuring over 30 films from Indigenous filmmakers across Canada, the United States, Australia, Brazil and Fiji with a free opening night screening to kick things off.

title: Edmonton Mayor Andrew Knack Departs on 11-Day Trade Mission to China and Japan
excerpt: Mayor Andrew Knack left Edmonton Saturday on an Edmonton Global-led trade mission to China and Japan, with the goal of drawing foreign investment into the city's manufacturing and clean energy sectors. The trip runs until April 15.
author: Culture Alberta

title: From Edmonton Motels to the NBA: How Onsi Saleh Is Turning the Atlanta Hawks Into a Contender
excerpt: Raised in Edmonton and educated at the University of Alberta, Onsi Saleh is now the general manager of the Atlanta Hawks and his first season running the show has the franchise on track for the playoffs after one of the most controversial trades in recent memory.

title: Edmonton Mayor Andrew Knack Names Former Federal NDP MP Blake Desjarlais as Chief of Staff
excerpt: Mayor Andrew Knack has appointed Blake Desjarlais a former NDP Member of Parliament for Edmonton-Griesbach as Chief of Staff for the Mayor's Office, effective April 7, 2026.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Edmonton Woman Missing Since August Partner Charged with Murder, Body Still Not Found
excerpt: Marika "Mika" Sutton, 29, was last seen in Mill Woods in August 2025. Five months later, her partner John Angus Joseph MacArthur, 47, was arrested in Charlottetown, P.E.I. and charged with second-degree murder. Her remains have not been located. Police believe her body was disposed of along Highway 2 between Wetaskiwin and Lacombe in late August 2025.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Edmonton's Winspear Centre Is Getting a Second Concert Hall Here's What's Being Built
excerpt: Edmonton's Winspear Centre expansion is finally getting finished. The new 550-seat Music Box concert hall is set to open fall 2027 here's what's being built and why it almost didn't happen.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Edmonton Man Missing Since 2020 May Have Been Spotted Near River Valley
excerpt: Edmonton police believe Jeffrey Caporale, missing since April 2020, may have been recently spotted near Groat Road and 102 Avenue but officers weren't able to confirm his identity before losing contact.

title: 19-Year-Old Edmonton Man Arrested in Calgary After Weeks on the Run for Two GTA Murders
excerpt: Isaiah Thomas Badger, 19, of Edmonton, was arrested in Calgary on March 19 after weeks on the run following a Canada-wide warrant for two separate murders in Vaughan and Etobicoke. Here's the full timeline.

title: Daryl Katz Net Worth 2026: Edmonton Oilers Owner Now Worth $7.2 Billion After Forbes Ranking
excerpt: Daryl Katz, Edmonton-born owner of the Edmonton Oilers, has a net worth of $7.2 billion according to the 2026 Forbes World's Billionaires List up $1.4 billion in a single year.

title: Edmonton's South Asian Community Has Been Targeted by Extortion for Years Two Men Finally Deported as 51 More Suspects Identified
excerpt: Edmonton police and the CBSA have deported two men and identified 51 additional suspects tied to a violent extortion network targeting South Asian business owners in Edmonton. The joint forces operation involving EPS, ALERT, RCMP, and CBSA is ongoing.
author: Culture Alberta

title: University of Alberta Women's Volleyball Team Brings Home First National Title in Nearly Two Decades
excerpt: The University of Alberta Pandas swept Sherbrooke 3-0 on Sunday to win the 2026 U Sports national women's volleyball championship their first title since 2007. Abby Guezen of Sherwood Park was named tournament MVP as the Pandas claimed their eighth national banner.

title: Edmonton Police Chief Defends Israel Trip and Pushes Back on His Critics
excerpt: Edmonton Police Chief Warren Driechel has broken his silence on the Israel trip that divided the city, defending the visit and pushing back on calls from community groups to account for it. It's his first public statement since Mayor Andrew Knack called him out last week and he's not backing down
author: Culture Alberta

title: Leon Draisaitl Out for the Rest of the Regular Season After Hit in Nashville Game
excerpt: Leon Draisaitl is out for the rest of the regular season after taking a hit from Nashville's Ozzy Wiesblatt on Sunday. The Oilers, sitting just four points ahead of the playoff cut line with 14 games left, now face a difficult stretch without their second-best player.

title: Edmonton Man Birinder Singh, 22, Identified in Fatal Weekend Shooting on the QEII
excerpt: Birinder Singh, 22, has been identified by family and friends as the Edmonton man shot and killed on Highway 2 south of Leduc on Saturday afternoon. RCMP's Major Crimes Unit is investigating and has no images of the suspect vehicle.

title: Driver Killed in Highway 2 Shooting Near Leduc Alberta
excerpt: A driver was shot and killed on Highway 2 near Leduc Saturday afternoon. Alberta RCMP Major Crimes is investigating and the suspect vehicle has not been identified.
